execution: Direct via Shopify Admin MCP (graphql_mutation metafieldsSet) — overwrote the stale Phase-pre-290 seo.title template ('X… in Los Angeles · LA Mattress' or 'X in Los Angeles · LA Mattress') on every active+published product where it was still in place. The Phase 290 rewrite of scripts/seo-backfill-product-seo.mjs introduced the new template (seo.title = product.title) but its idempotency rule only touched null values, so existing stale titles never got replaced. This sweep finishes the migration.
stale_pattern_matched: seo.title ending in (or containing) ` in Los Angeles · LA Mattress` — with or without the truncation ellipsis `…`. Both forms are the legacy auto-generated template that the May 15 SEMrush re-audit flagged for 'Duplicate title tag' across 39 products.

examples_before_after.before: Diamond Rock Extra Firm 12"… in Los Angeles · LA Mattress
Southerland Cobalt Extra Firm… in Los Angeles · LA Mattress
Rize Home Deluxe Bed Frame in Los Angeles · LA Mattress
Tempur-Pedic… in Los Angeles · LA Mattress
Queen Eclipse Ice Tufted Firm… in Los Angeles · LA Mattress
examples_before_after.after: Queen Eclipse Ice Tufted Firm Euro Top 15" Floor Sample Clearance Mattress

preserved_data: seo.description was NOT touched on any of these products. The stale template only existed in seo.title, and the corresponding description_tag metafields contain longer / more informative copy that didn't have the duplicate-title-tag SERP issue.
preserved_non_stale_titles: Products with seo.title containing '| LA Mattress Store' suffix or other merchant-authored patterns were left alone (potentially intentional merchant overrides). Only the exact 'in Los Angeles · LA Mattress' pattern was treated as stale.
verification: Sampled 3 products via product(id:){seo}; all 3 return the new title (full product.title, no suffix). Phase 290 idempotency rule still applies for future writes — re-running won't re-touch these because the seo.title is no longer in the stale form.
seo_impact: SEMrush 'Duplicate title tag' SERP flag should clear within the next crawl cycle (typically 2-7 days for Google to re-index). Each title is now unique to its product, which directly improves SERP CTR for variant family products (Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt Soft / Medium / Firm, Diamond Diana Firm / Plush, etc.) that previously truncated to identical strings.
